operating_state: Revenue reached $35.1 billion, up 94% year-over-year and 17% sequentially, driven by Data Center platforms generating 112% year-over-year growth from Hopper architecture demand for large language model training and inference. Cloud service providers represented approximately 50% of Data Center revenue with strong growth across all customer types. Gaming revenue increased while Professional Visualization and Automotive segments showed modest growth. The company continues transitioning from Hopper to Blackwell architecture with initial Blackwell shipments beginning in Q4.
cost_structure: Gross margin decreased sequentially to 73.0% from 75.1% due to mix shift to more complex Data Center systems and inventory provisions for low-yielding Blackwell material, though remained above prior year driven by favorable Data Center product mix. Operating expenses increased 44% year-over-year driven by employee expansion, compute infrastructure costs, and engineering development costs for accelerated product roadmap transitions.
material_events: Board approved additional $50 billion share repurchase authorization and appointed Ellen Ochoa as new director. Company repurchased $11.1 billion of shares in Q3 and $26.2 billion in first nine months of fiscal 2025. Completed successful mask change for Blackwell architecture improving production yields. NVIDIA transitioned certain testing, validation, and supply operations out of China and Hong Kong following export control implementations.
macro_exposures: USG export controls continue restricting shipments of high-performance products including A100, H100, and Blackwell systems to China and other specified regions, with no licenses received through the NAC process. Geopolitical conflict in Israel affects approximately 4,300 employees primarily supporting networking product development, with substantial numbers called for military duty potentially disrupting operations. Supply chain constraints affect both Hopper and Blackwell systems with critical suppliers and manufacturing partners concentrated in Taiwan.
forward_outlook: Blackwell production shipments scheduled to begin Q4 fiscal 2025 with continued ramp into fiscal 2026, though demand expected to exceed supply for several quarters. Company implementing annual Data Center architecture cadence with greater product variety to meet diverse AI opportunities while expanding supply chain capacity through prepaid manufacturing agreements and new supplier relationships.
uncertainty_profile: Regulatory inquiries from multiple jurisdictions including EU, US, UK, China, and South Korea regarding GPU sales, supply allocation, and AI partnerships create ongoing compliance burdens with potential new legislation imposing additional operational restrictions. Complex product transitions requiring simultaneous shipping of multiple architectures create demand prediction challenges and inventory management complexity. Export control rules remain subject to frequent changes with potential expansion to broader product categories and geographic scope.
